notes: MIL-DTL-46100 Class 3 high-hardness armor steel. 534-578 BHN. RHAe ~1.35-1.45 per mm vs AP per ARL-TR-4632. Higher hardness gives better ballistic protection but reduced ductility and increased HAZ embrittlement risk. Limited formability.

source.methodology: RHAe interpolated from ARL-TR-4632 hardness-to-RHAe correlation. Hardness range per MIL-DTL-46100 Class 3 specification.
